parsed_plan.title: Plan: Add `inspect` CLI subcommand to aco_runtime_lib
parsed_plan.goal: Provide a `python -m aco_runtime_lib inspect <wf_id>` CLI subcommand that reads a workflow's persisted JSONL log and prints its history (summary, plan, task statuses, and chronological events). Support `--summary`, `--events`, and `--plan` flags for section selection, and ship pytest tests covering the new functionality.
parsed_plan.architecture:
- The existing `__main__.py` uses `argparse` with subparsers; add a new `inspect` subparser.
- New module `aco_runtime_lib/inspector.py` exposes:
  - `load_log(wf_id, log_dir=None) -> Iterator[dict]` ˇŞ streams parsed JSONL events.
  - `build_report(wf_id, log_dir=None) -> Report` ˇŞ aggregates events into a structured report.
  - `format_summary(report)`, `format_plan(report)`, `format_events(report)` ˇŞ render each section for stdout.
- The `inspect` subcommand wires argparse ˇú `build_report` ˇú the selected `format_*` calls.
- Log file location follows the existing convention (e.g., `logs/<wf_id>.jsonl`); reuse the current path resolver if one exists, otherwise introduce a small resolver in `inspector.py`.
- Tests live in `tests/test_inspect.py` using `tmp_path` for synthetic JSONL logs and `capsys` / `subprocess` for CLI assertions.

nodes.title: Locate existing CLI entry point, argparse pattern, and JSONL log path resolver
Add `inspect` subparser to `__main__.py` and wire `--summary`, `--events`, `--plan` flags
Implement section formatters (summary, plan, events) with stable output
Write pytest tests covering module functions, each flag, defaults, and error paths
Document the new subcommand in README and CLI reference

data_model.name: Summary
data_model.description: `{status, started_at, ended_at, duration_s, total_tasks, completed, failed}`

acceptance.description: `python -m aco_runtime_lib inspect <wf_id>` runs and prints summary, plan, and events sections in that order when no flags are supplied.
Each of `--summary`, `--events`, `--plan` prints only its section when supplied alone; multiple flags combine and the sections are printed in summary ˇú plan ˇú events order.
The subcommand appears in `python -m aco_runtime_lib inspect --help` with usage examples.
An unknown `wf_id` (missing log file) exits with code `3` and prints a clear error to stderr.
A malformed JSONL line is reported with its line number on stderr, and processing continues for subsequent lines; exit code is `4` only if no usable events could be extracted.
Task statuses reflect the latest event per task (`started` ˇú `completed` or `failed`); tasks never started remain `pending`.
Summary correctly computes total/completed/failed counts and duration from `workflow_start` / `workflow_end` timestamps.

`pytest tests/test_inspect.py` passes with coverage of: empty log, full happy-path log, partial log (no `workflow_end`), missing log file, malformed line, each flag combination, default (no flags) output.
README documents the subcommand, its flags, default behavior, and exit codes.

out_of_scope: Live tailing or streaming of in-progress workflows.
Filtering events by type, time range, or task id.
Exporting reports to JSON, HTML, or other formats.
Mutating, rewriting, or compacting the log.
Authentication or access control on log files.
A `--tail N`, `--since`, `--until`, or `--json` output mode (left for a follow-up).
